intent.symptom_summary: TabBar tabs flex-grow with their title text: a long title stretches the tab across the strip and pushes the new-tab (+) button. Tabs should hold a fixed max-width and ellipsis-truncate; tab width must stay stable and the + button stay anchored.
intent.desired_summary: Cap the tab cell so it never grows with title length and long titles truncate with an ellipsis. Concretely: add max-width:220px to .tabbar .tabs__tab-wrapper (the design's .tab equivalent), matching design-fidelity-contract section 5. The .tabs__tab-label ellipsis machinery (white-space:nowrap + overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + flex:1) is already present and fires once the cap exists; the button already has min-width:0 + flex:1 1 auto. Tab width stays stable; +/spacer/overflow-chevron actions stay anchored. Computed-style fidelity AC targets per contract: wrapper max-width resolves 220px, padding 0 10px 0 12px, gap 8px, tab bar height 36px; var(--token) asserted as resolves-to-token (not literal hex).
intent.verbatim_prompt: A browser-style tab in an HTTP client's tab strip grows wider with its title instead of staying a fixed width — a long title stretches the tab across the strip and pushes the new-tab (+) button. Desired: each tab has a max-width with white-space:nowrap + text-overflow:ellipsis and does NOT reflow or grow with title length — long titles truncate to an ellipsis, tab width stays stable, the + button stays in place. Zero behaviour change: tab select/close/reorder, the dirty dot, the method chip, active-tab styling all stay; this is a pure CSS/layout constraint on the tab title. Scope: the TabBar / tab-strip (specs/002-tabs-primitive, specs/005-tab-bar-visual-fidelity), NOT the RequestBar. Visual fidelity is MANDATORY: design/reference.html is the VISUAL TARGET to match to pixel parity, and the exact resolved values are pre-extracted in design/styles.css and design/design-fidelity-contract.md (section 'Tab strip' — notably .tab max-width 220px and .tab-label white-space:nowrap/overflow:hidden/text-overflow:ellipsis, plus tab padding/gap/height) — use those exact computed-style values as the fidelity AC targets, treating var(--token) as resolves-to-token (not a literal hex). Copy the VALUES, never the CODE: the export carries generated cruft (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, a tweaks-panel) that must NOT enter the build — rebuild with semantic class names bound to design/tokens.json. unchanged_behaviour: tab select/close/reorder, dirty dot, method chip, active styling, title text content (only overflow rendering changes); tests stay green.

plan_seeds.recommended_approach_summary: Reuses the canonical truncation rule already present at src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Tabs.css:196 (it matches design-fidelity-contract B1) instead of writing any new styling, and derives its authority from design-fidelity-contract section 5. The single cell-level cap resolves the tab-growth symptom under every enumerated framing, so no flex-layer change is needed. It satisfies unchanged_behavior: the change is .tabbar-scoped and CSS-only, so select/close/reorder, the dirty dot, the method chip, active styling, and the rendered text stay byte-identical; the sole visible difference is how an overlong title is clipped. Bare .tabs consumers are untouched. Runtime behaviour is deferred to the computed-style probe captured under Open Uncertainties before finalizing.

alternatives_considered.summary: Change .tabbar .tabs__tab-label max-width from 200px to 220px in place (TabBar.css:88); leave the cap on the label.
